auditSummary: Full codebase audit found 34 bugs (5 critical, 12 high, 12 medium, 5 low), 9 documentation inaccuracies, 11 test integrity issues, 6 TigerStyle violations, and 13 missing LSP capabilities compared to rust-analyzer/gopls. 1588 tests pass but ~15% have vacuous assertions.

stories.title: Fix extractInitializerType returning function name instead of type
stories.description: As a Pike developer using the LSP, I want type inference from call expressions to resolve to the actual return type, so that completion and hover show correct types. Currently Dog d = makeDog() sets assignedType to 'makeDog' (the function name), not 'Dog' (the return type).
acceptanceCriteria: extractInitializerType in symbolTable.ts does not assign the callee function name as assignedType
For Dog d = makeDog(), assignedType is either resolved to Dog (if resolvable) or left unset — never 'makeDog'
Add a test: parse 'class Dog {} Dog d = makeDog();' and verify assignedType is not 'makeDog'
All existing tests pass (1588+)
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-001. Key file: server/src/features/symbolTable.ts extractInitializerType(). The function drills through postfix_expr to find the callee name for call expressions, but returns the callee FUNCTION name instead of the type. Fix: do not assign assignedType from call expressions unless the return type can be statically resolved.

stories.title: Fix PikeWorker requestCount never resetting after forced restart
stories.description: As a developer, I want the PikeWorker request counter to reset after a forced restart, so that the worker doesn't restart on every subsequent request. After shouldForceRestart() triggers, requestCount stays above ceiling forever.
acceptanceCriteria: requestCount is reset to 0 in restart() method after the new process is confirmed healthy
Add a test: force requestCount above ceiling, trigger restart, verify it resets to 0
Worker does not restart on every request after a forced restart
All existing tests pass
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-002. Key file: server/src/features/pikeWorker.ts. shouldForceRestart() checks requestCount against maxRequestsBeforeRestart, but restart() never resets requestCount to 0. Add this.requestCount = 0 after successful ping in restart().

stories.title: Replace PikeWorker queue Array.shift with proper deque
stories.description: As a developer, I want the PikeWorker request queue to process in O(1) per dequeue, so that rapid edit bursts don't create visible latency. Array.shift() is O(n) per call, making total queue processing O(n^2).
acceptanceCriteria: Replace this.queue.shift() with index-based dequeue (maintain head index, reset when empty)
Or use a simple ring buffer pattern
Existing worker tests still pass
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-003. Key file: server/src/features/pikeWorker.ts drainQueue(). Replace const item = this.queue.shift() with const item = this.queue[this.head++]; if (this.head >= this.queue.length) { this.queue = []; this.head = 0; }. Simple index-based approach, no external dependency.

stories.title: Encode file URIs in backgroundIndex
stories.description: As a Pike developer with spaces in file paths, I want background indexing to produce valid URIs so that workspace symbols are discoverable. Currently file://${filepath} does not encode spaces or special characters.
acceptanceCriteria: backgroundIndex.ts uses encodeURI or a proper URI constructor for file URIs
Add a test: create temp directory with space in name, verify workspace/symbol returns valid URI with %20 encoding
Existing background index tests pass
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-004. Key file: server/src/features/backgroundIndex.ts. Replace `file://${filepath}` with a proper URI encoding function. Consider using vscode-uri's URI.file() if available, or manual encodeURIComponent on path segments.

stories.title: Convert moduleResolver sync filesystem calls to async
stories.description: As a developer, I want module resolution to not block the event loop, so that LSP startup and file resolution don't freeze the server. detectPikePaths uses execSync, existsSync, statSync — all block the event loop during onInitialize.
acceptanceCriteria: detectPikePaths uses execFile (async) instead of execSync
findModuleInPath uses fs.promises.access and fs.promises.stat instead of sync variants
onInitialize handler awaits the async path detection
All callers of moduleResolver methods updated to handle async
Existing module resolver tests pass
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-005. Key file: server/src/features/moduleResolver.ts. This is the worst offender for event loop blocking. detectPikePaths is called during WorkspaceIndex construction which happens in onInitialize. Convert detectPikePaths to async, make resolve* methods async, update all callers to await.

stories.title: Fix declForHover signature extraction including trailing semicolons
stories.description: As a Pike developer using hover, I want to see clean function/class signatures without trailing semicolons or comments. Currently declForHover extracts the full source line which includes trailing junk.
acceptanceCriteria: declForHover in server.ts extracts the declaration node range from the tree-sitter AST instead of splitting source by newline
Signatures do not include trailing ;, {, or inline comments
Add a test: hover over 'int x = 5; // comment' and verify signature is 'int x = 5' not 'int x = 5; // comment'
Existing hover tests pass
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-006. Key file: server/src/server.ts declForHover(). Currently uses source.split('\n')[decl.range.start.line] which grabs the entire line. Instead, use the tree-sitter node's range to extract only the declaration text. For nodes without precise ranges, trim trailing semicolons and whitespace.

stories.title: Deduplicate and fix scope-stripping regex for nested scope() calls
stories.description: As a developer, I want a single correct scope-stripping utility, so that nested scope() calls render correctly and the logic isn't duplicated between server.ts and completion.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: Extract scope-stripping logic to a shared utility function in server/src/util/
Handle nested scope() calls correctly (e.g., scope(0, scope(1, function(...))))
Replace both usages in server.ts renderPredefSignature and completion.ts cleanPredefSignature
Add tests for: simple scope(), nested scope(), scope with complex inner types, no scope wrapper
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-007. The naive regex breaks on nested scope() calls. Need a recursive descent approach: parse the scope(...) wrapper, strip it, then check if the result is also a scope wrapper and strip that too. Key files: server/src/server.ts renderPredefSignature, server/src/features/completion.ts cleanPredefSignature.

stories.title: Distinguish dot vs arrow member access in completion
stories.description: As a Pike developer, I want the LSP to respect Pike's access control — '.' shows only public members, '->' shows all members. Currently completeMemberAccess ignores the accessType parameter entirely.
acceptanceCriteria: completeMemberAccess uses the accessType parameter (currently _accessType, ignored)
For '.' access, filter to public declarations only (no private/protected modifier)
For '->' access, show all declarations regardless of visibility
Add tests for both access types on a class with mixed public/private members
Existing completion tests pass
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-008. Key file: server/src/features/completion.ts completeMemberAccess(). The parameter is already there but prefixed with underscore (ignored). Remove the underscore prefix and add visibility filtering. Pike access modifiers: public (default), private, protected, static. Tree-sitter should capture these on declarations.

stories.title: Fix typeResolver synthetic Declaration ID collisions and remove dead code
stories.description: As a developer, I want synthetic declarations to have unique IDs, so that declById lookups don't collide. Currently all synthetic declarations use id: -1. Also remove the dead containsDecl function.
acceptanceCriteria: Use a monotonically decreasing counter for synthetic declaration IDs (starting from -1, decrementing)
Remove dead containsDecl function from typeResolver.ts
Existing type resolution tests pass
Add a test: create multiple synthetic declarations and verify they have different IDs in declById
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-010. Key file: server/src/features/typeResolver.ts resolveQualifiedType(). Creates synthetic Declaration with id: -1, colliding if multiple exist. Fix: let syntheticId = -1; then use syntheticId-- for each new synthetic. The containsDecl function is defined but never called.

stories.title: Fix signatureHelp for chained calls and nested function types
stories.description: As a Pike developer using signature help, I want correct parameter tracking for chained calls and function-type parameters. Currently findEnclosingCall doesn't verify cursor position inside args, and splitParams breaks on function type signatures.
acceptanceCriteria: findEnclosingCall verifies cursor is inside the argument list (between '(' and matching ')')
For chained calls a.b().c(), correctly identifies c as the callee
splitParams handles nested function type signatures function(int, string: void) as a single parameter
Add tests for chained calls and function-type parameters
Existing signature help tests pass
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-011, A-012. Key file: server/src/features/signatureHelp.ts. findEnclosingCall should track paren depth to verify cursor is inside args. splitParams should track paren/bracket depth and not split on commas inside nested function types.

stories.title: Fix accessResolver chained access and redundant re-parsing
stories.description: As a developer, I want chained access a.b.c to resolve correctly, and I want accessResolver to accept a pre-parsed tree to avoid redundant parsing.
acceptanceCriteria: resolveAccessCore accepts optional pre-parsed tree parameter to avoid re-parsing
Chained access correctly unwraps nested postfix_expr nodes
Add tests for a.b.c, a->b->c, and mixed a.b->c patterns
Existing access resolver and definition/hover tests pass
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-013, A-014. Key file: server/src/features/accessResolver.ts. For a.b.c, the postfix_expr is nested — children[i-1] may be another postfix_expr rather than an identifier. Need to recursively unwrap. resolveAccessCore parses the document again even though the caller already has a parsed tree — add an optional tree parameter.

stories.title: Fix workspaceIndex hash sign and cross-file false positives
stories.description: As a developer, I want content hashes to always be positive hex strings and cross-file references to not produce false positives from same-name symbols in different files.
acceptanceCriteria: hashContent uses >>> 0 (unsigned right shift) to ensure unsigned 32-bit result
getCrossFileReferences adds file-path scoping to prevent same-name symbol false positives
Existing workspace index tests pass
Add a test: two files with unrelated same-name symbols, verify getCrossFileReferences does not match them
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-015, A-016. Key file: server/src/features/workspaceIndex.ts. hashContent's & 0xffffffff produces signed int32 in JS. Use (hash >>> 0).toString(16) instead. getCrossFileReferences matches by name with ref.resolvesTo === null — needs file-path scoping.

stories.title: Fix PikeWorker restart health check race condition
stories.description: As a developer, I want worker restart to be reliable, so that the server recovers cleanly from worker crashes. Currently restart() waits a fixed 100ms then pings — the process may not be ready.
acceptanceCriteria: restart() replaces fixed 100ms sleep with a health-check loop (ping with exponential backoff, max 3 attempts, 500ms total)
If health check fails after max attempts, surface error to client via window/showMessage
Add a test for restart health check success and failure
Existing worker tests pass
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-017. Key file: server/src/features/pikeWorker.ts restart(). Replace setTimeout(100) with a retry loop: for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) { try { await ping(); return; } catch { await sleep(100 * (i + 1)); } }. Surface failure via connection.window.showErrorMessage().

stories.title: Fix rename reserved pattern and protected name validation
stories.description: As a developer, I want rename to correctly reject all reserved identifiers and check protected names at validation time, not just at getRenameLocations time.
acceptanceCriteria: __foo__ regex uses /^__[a-zA-Z].*__$/ to match uppercase patterns like __FOO__
validateRenameName checks against protectedNames set, not just PIKE_KEYWORDS
Add tests for __FOO__ rejection and protected name rejection at validation time
Existing rename tests pass
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-025, A-026. Key file: server/src/features/rename.ts. The regex /^__[a-z].*__$/ misses uppercase. validateRenameName only checks PIKE_KEYWORDS, not the protectedNames set that gets checked later in getRenameLocations.

stories.title: Fix parser cache LRU recency update
stories.description: As a developer, I want cached parse trees to have their recency updated on access, so that frequently-used trees aren't prematurely evicted.
acceptanceCriteria: getCachedTree in parser.ts calls the LRU get method instead of raw map access
Add a test: fill cache to capacity, access oldest entry, add new entry, verify oldest entry is NOT evicted
Existing parser tests pass
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-018. Key file: server/src/parser.ts getCachedTree(). Currently uses treeCache.entries.get(key)?.tree which bypasses the LRU's get() method that updates recency. Change to use the LRU cache's proper get method.

stories.title: Add WASM load error reporting
stories.description: As a developer debugging a broken installation, I want to see which WASM candidate paths were tried and why each failed, instead of silent swallowing.
acceptanceCriteria: initParser logs each candidate path and the error before trying the next
If all candidates fail, throws an error listing all attempted paths and their errors
Add a test: all candidate paths fail, verify the error message lists all paths
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-019. Key file: server/src/parser.ts initParser(). Currently catches all errors silently. Collect errors in an array during the candidate loop, then throw a descriptive error if none succeed.

stories.title: Clarify getReferencesTo return semantics
stories.description: As a developer consuming the symbol table API, I want getReferencesTo to return only actual references, not include the declaration itself via unshift, so that callers don't have to know the first element is special.
acceptanceCriteria: getReferencesTo returns only actual references, not the declaration itself
Audit all callers — update any that relied on the declaration being included
Add or update a test verifying getReferencesTo returns only references
Existing reference tests pass
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-020. Key file: server/src/features/symbolTable.ts getReferencesTo(). Currently does results.unshift(decl.range) to include the declaration. This is surprising behavior. Remove the unshift. Check all callers in server.ts to see if any rely on the declaration being first.

stories.title: Remove unreachable duplicate case in autodocRenderer
stories.description: As a developer, I want no unreachable code in the autodoc renderer.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove duplicate case 'group' in renderBlocks in autodocRenderer.ts
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-024. Key file: server/src/features/autodocRenderer.ts renderBlocks(). Has two case 'group' handlers — the second is unreachable dead code.

stories.title: Replace diagnosticManager SHA-256 with fast hash and guard line conversion
stories.description: As a developer, I want content hashing to be fast and Pike-to-LSP line conversion to be safe against edge cases.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace SHA-256 in computeContentHash with a fast non-crypto hash (FNV-1a or DJB2)
Guard pd.line - 1 conversion in mergeDiagnostics against pd.line <= 0
Existing diagnostic tests pass
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-027, A-028. Key file: server/src/features/diagnosticManager.ts. SHA-256 is overkill for cache invalidation — use a simple fast hash. The line conversion pd.line - 1 can produce negative values if Pike reports line 0.

stories.title: Fix persistentCache ESM/CJS mismatch and sync read
stories.description: As a developer, I want cache code to work in both Bun and strict ESM environments, and not block the event loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace require('node:fs') with import { readFileSync } from 'node:fs'
Or convert to async readFile to avoid blocking the event loop
Existing cache tests pass
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding A-029. Key file: server/src/features/persistentCache.ts computeWasmHash(). Uses require('node:fs') (CommonJS) in an ESM module. Fix with a top-level import. Also consider making it async.

stories.title: Fix all documentation inaccuracies
stories.description: As a developer reading the docs, I want every document to accurately reflect the current state of the codebase.
acceptanceCriteria: AGENTS.md: change extension/ to client/ in project structure
AGENTS.md: remove 'bun run lint' from build instructions or add a lint script to package.json
docs/architecture.md: change extension/ to client/
docs/ci.md: replace npm ci and setup-node with Bun equivalents
docs/existing-tooling.md: add note that tree-sitter-pike now exists, mark 'no grammar' claim as historical
docs/state-of-project.md: update test count to 1588, corpus count to 66
decisions/0002-tier-3-scope.md: update 'out of scope' to note rename and code actions were implemented
decisions/0010-cross-file-resolution.md: remove 'NOT IMPLEMENTED' note for resolve.pike
decisions/0003-pike-ai-kb-integration.md: add note that PikeOracle MCP was replaced by PikeWorker subprocess per decision 0011
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit findings D-001 through D-009. Documentation-only story, no code changes. All 9 inaccuracies fixed in one pass.

stories.title: Remove mock from hover tests
stories.description: As a developer, I want hover tests to use real Pike responses, not monkey-patched worker methods. The hover.test.ts US-009 tests violate the project's no-mock rule.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace worker.typeof_ = async (...) => ... with real Pike subprocess invocation (gated on pikeAvailable)
Add describe.skipIf(!pikeAvailable) for typeof tests
Or: use pre-recorded Pike responses from harness snapshots as fixtures
No monkey-patching of server internals
All hover tests pass when Pike is available
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding T-001. Key file: tests/lsp/hover.test.ts. The typeof_ monkey-patch simulates Pike responses. Replace with either real Pike invocation (skipIf unavailable) or pre-recorded JSON fixtures from harness snapshots.

stories.title: Harden vacuous conditional assertions in test suite
stories.description: As a developer, I want tests that fail when the tested behavior is wrong, not tests that pass silently. Multiple tests use 'if (result) { expect... }' patterns that pass vacuously when result is null/undefined.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit all tests in references.test.ts, edge-cases.test.ts, definition.test.ts for if (result) { expect... } patterns
Replace with expect(result).toBeDefined() followed by unconditional expect(result!.xxx)
Or use assert(result) before expectations
Verify no test count change (same tests, stronger assertions)
All tests pass
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding T-002, T-003, T-004. Key files: tests/lsp/references.test.ts, tests/lsp/edge-cases.test.ts, tests/lsp/definition.test.ts. About 15% of tests have these patterns. The fix is mechanical but requires reading each test to determine the correct non-vacuous assertion.

stories.title: Remove or implement integration test stubs
stories.description: As a developer, I want no empty test files inflating the file count. Four integration test files export nothing.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete tests/integration/activation.test.ts (empty stub)
Delete tests/integration/documentSymbol.test.ts (empty stub)
Delete tests/integration/error-recovery.test.ts (empty stub)
For tests/integration/crossFilePropagation.test.ts: implement real cross-file propagation tests using existing corpus files and PikeWorker, or delete it
Run full test suite — verify 0 fail
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit findings T-005, T-006, T-007, T-008. Three files are 9-11 lines of comments with export {}. crossFilePropagation.test.ts has 45 lines of comments describing what to test but no implementation. Decision: delete the 3 minimal stubs; for crossFilePropagation, either implement or delete.

stories.title: Implement lifecycle todo test
stories.description: As a developer, I want the exit-after-shutdown test to exist and pass. Currently lifecycle.test.ts has one test.todo that was never implemented.
acceptanceCriteria: Convert test.todo('exit after shutdown does not throw') to a real test
Test sends shutdown request, then exit notification, verifies no thrown error
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding T-009. Key file: tests/lsp/lifecycle.test.ts. Simple test: await client.sendRequest('shutdown'), then send 'exit' notification, verify clean shutdown.

stories.title: Add correctness assertions to real-codebase-verification tests
stories.description: As a developer, I want the corpus-wide smoke tests to verify semantic correctness, not just 'didn't crash.' Currently 11 tests mostly check toBeDefined().
acceptanceCriteria: For each corpus file, verify at least one semantic property: symbol count matches expected range, or specific symbol names are present
Verify hover returns non-empty markdown for at least one symbol per file
Verify definition returns a valid location for at least one identifier per file
Existing tests pass with stronger assertions
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Audit finding T-010. Key file: tests/lsp/real-codebase-verification.test.ts. Currently crash-only testing. Add semantic spot-checks that verify actual content, not just existence. Reference: the existing documentSymbol.test.ts already does corpus-wide name coverage checks.

stories.title: Split symbolTable.ts from 1892 lines to multiple focused modules
stories.description: As a developer, I want the symbol table module to be within TigerStyle 500-line limits, so that each file has a single responsibility. Currently 3.8x over the limit.
acceptanceCriteria: Extract scope construction into scopeBuilder.ts
Extract declaration collection into declarationCollector.ts
Extract reference collection into referenceCollector.ts
Extract cross-file wiring into crossFileResolver.ts
Keep core types and buildSymbolTable orchestrator in symbolTable.ts
All resulting files are <= 500 lines
All existing tests pass with only import path changes
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: TigerStyle violation. Key file: server/src/features/symbolTable.ts (1892 lines). Deferred to a follow-up phase per user preference. The 4-pass buildSymbolTable architecture naturally splits along pass boundaries.

stories.title: Split server.ts from 1087 lines to multiple handler modules
stories.description: As a developer, I want the server entry point to be within TigerStyle 500-line limits. Currently 2.2x over the limit.
acceptanceCriteria: Extract hover handler logic into hoverHandler.ts
Extract completion handler wiring into completionHandler.ts
Extract definition/references handlers into navigationHandler.ts
Keep createPikeServer wiring and lifecycle in server.ts
All resulting files are <= 500 lines
All existing tests pass
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: TigerStyle violation. Key file: server/src/server.ts (1087 lines). Deferred phase. Split along handler boundaries — each LSP feature's handler logic goes into its own file.

stories.title: Split completion.ts from 927 lines to focused modules
stories.description: As a developer, I want completion to be within TigerStyle 500-line limits. Currently 1.9x over the limit.
acceptanceCriteria: Extract detectTriggerContext (~200 lines) into completionTrigger.ts
Extract member/stdlib completion providers into completionProviders.ts
Keep core getCompletions orchestrator in completion.ts
All resulting files are <= 500 lines
All existing completion tests pass
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: TigerStyle violation. Key file: server/src/features/completion.ts (927 lines). Deferred phase. The detectTriggerContext function alone is ~200 lines of AST pattern matching.

stories.title: Split autodocRenderer.ts from 918 lines to focused modules
stories.description: As a developer, I want the autodoc renderer to be within TigerStyle 500-line limits. Currently 1.8x over the limit.
acceptanceCriteria: Extract XML parser into xmlParser.ts
Extract block/inline rendering into autodocBlocks.ts
Keep main render entry point in autodocRenderer.ts
All resulting files are <= 500 lines
All existing autodoc tests pass
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: TigerStyle violation. Key file: server/src/features/autodocRenderer.ts (918 lines). Deferred phase. Clean split: XML parsing is self-contained, rendering is the bulk.

stories.title: Add immutable workspace snapshot pattern
stories.description: As a developer, I want each LSP request to operate on a consistent snapshot of workspace state, so that concurrent edits don't produce inconsistent results. Inspired by gopls's Snapshot type.
acceptanceCriteria: Design a WorkspaceSnapshot type that holds immutable references to: parse trees, symbol tables, workspace index, PikeWorker cache
Each didChange creates a new snapshot; in-flight requests reference the old one
Handlers receive snapshot as parameter instead of accessing mutable globals
Reference: gopls internal/cache/snapshot.go
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Missing capability M-001. Long-term architectural improvement inspired by gopls. Current mutable state lives in: documents map (server.ts), autodocCache (LRU), workspaceIndex (WorkspaceIndex), pikeWorker (PikeWorker). This is the biggest architectural gap vs mature LSPs.

stories.title: Add CancellationToken propagation to PikeWorker
stories.description: As a developer, I want cancellation requests to abort Pike subprocess work, so that stale diagnostics don't consume resources. Currently cancellation is only checked at the handler level, not propagated to the worker.
acceptanceCriteria: PikeWorker.enqueue accepts an optional CancellationToken
Check token before writing to stdin; if cancelled, resolve immediately with empty result
Reference: gopls context-based cancellation
Existing worker tests pass
Add a test: enqueue with already-cancelled token returns immediately
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Missing capability M-013. Key file: server/src/features/pikeWorker.ts. The FIFO queue should check the cancellation token before processing each queued item. If cancelled, skip and resolve with empty result.

stories.title: Add inlay hints support
stories.description: As a Pike developer, I want to see inferred types displayed inline, so that I can understand code without hovering. Inspired by rust-analyzer's inlay hints.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement textDocument/inlayHint handler
Show inferred types for mixed declarations where assignedType is resolved
Show parameter names at call sites when available from declarations
Register inlayHintProvider capability in initialize response
Add 5+ tests: type hint on mixed var, parameter name at call site, no hint on typed var, chained inference hint, disabled by configuration
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Missing capability M-003. New feature. Key files: server/src/server.ts (handler registration), new file server/src/features/inlayHints.ts. Reference: rust-analyzer inlay hints implementation. Uses existing assignedType and typeResolver infrastructure.

stories.title: Add go-to-implementation support
stories.description: As a Pike developer, I want to find all implementations of a class method or navigate inheritance hierarchies. Inspired by rust-analyzer and gopls.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement textDocument/implementation handler
For a class, find all classes that inherit from it across the workspace
For a method, find all overrides in subclasses
Uses existing WorkspaceIndex dependency graph and cross-file resolution
Register implementationProvider capability in initialize response
Add 5+ tests: class implementers, method overrides, no implementers, cross-file implementation, interface-like pattern
Typecheck passes
stories.notes: Missing capability M-005, M-006. New feature. Key files: server/src/server.ts (handler), server/src/features/workspaceIndex.ts (dependency graph). The workspace index already tracks inherit dependencies — walk the reverse dependency graph to find implementers.
